medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Began experiencing visual loss at age 22, diagnosed with retinal dystrophy. Three years later, had recurrent generalized tonic–clonic seizures and spontaneous myoclonic jerks resistant to barbiturates and valproate. At age 29, visual impairment severe but still able to work. Experienced transient but protracted visual disturbances consistent with palinopsia.
medical_history.family_history: Parents and paternal uncle heterozygous for the mutated gene, no signs of cognitive impairment. Early death in many older family members may have masked a likely autosomal-dominant pattern.

physical_examination.general_examination: Visual acuity greatly reduced (1/20), mild ataxia.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: EEG showed 8 Hz background activity and rare paroxysms of slow waves, often preceded by small spikes prominent over the parietal regions. MRI revealed selective and severe cerebellar atrophy. Skin biopsy revealed polymorphic lysosomal storage in glandular and endothelial cells. Electron microscopy of peripheral blood leukocytes showed fingerprint profiles in 1/100 lymphocytes.

laboratory_examinations.procedure_name: Electron microscopy of peripheral blood leukocytes

laboratory_examinations.findings: Polymorphic lysosomal storage in glandular and endothelial cells, cytoplasmic vacuoles with electron-dense 70–100 Å periodic structures (fingerprints) or flaky material (cotton-like storage), absence of progranulin protein in peripheral tissue

other_tests.findings: 8 Hz background activity and rare paroxysms of slow waves, often preceded by small spikes that were prominent over the parietal regions
Depressed cortical components, distorted waveform with a prominent P1 followed by broad negativity, virtually absent electroretinographic response
other_tests.changes_over_time: Further decline in the cortical components and appearance of polyphasic waves superimposed on P1 after recurrent episodes of palinopsia

other_tests.findings: Polyphasic cortical components at the time of visual impairment and onset of recurrent episodes of palinopsia
Pigment epithelium dystrophy and stippling, vessel attenuation, and optic nerve pallor
Central and peripheral retinal epithelium dystrophy and stippled hyperfluorescence
Retinal thinning and disruption of the retinal pigment epithelium
Borderline executive functions (frontal assessment battery 13.2; cutoff score 16)

diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis due to progranulin gene mutation (CLN11)
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was based on the presence of progressive retinopathy, recurrent generalized seizures, moderate ataxia, and subtle cognitive dysfunction in two siblings. Key evidence included retinal dystrophy, polyphasic visual-evoked potential waveform suggesting hyperexcitability of the occipital cortex, selective cerebellar atrophy on MRI, and fingerprint storage material in skin biopsy with absence of progranulin protein.
